mnemonics: HOWEVER: Handling Output With Error Validation & Efficient Recovery
keyPoints: Error handling is a crucial phase that allows the compiler to continue processing the source code after encountering an error.
It involves detecting an error, reporting it clearly to the user, and then recovering from it to find subsequent errors.
Common recovery strategies include Panic-mode, Phrase-level, Error productions, and Global-correction.
commonMistakes: Halting compilation completely after the first error, instead of recovering to find more.
Providing vague error messages that don't help the programmer locate and fix the issue.
